text: Enhance Usability of the Educational Modeling Software Apollon

text: Collaboration-aware modeling and a maintained Artemis integration

textPreview: with mobile and VS Code support, plus Athena model compatibility

text:
Supervisor: Felix Dietrich, M.Sc.
Examiner: Prof. Dr. Stephan Krusche
Munich | July 2026
textPreview: Supervisor: Felix Dietrich, M.Sc. | Examiner: Prof. Dr. Stephan Krusche | Munich | July 2026

text: 0:00-0:15. Open with the outcome: this thesis made collaboration visible and moved Artemis onto maintained Apollon while keeping teaching workflows intact. Mobile, VS Code, and Athena are supporting ecosystem results. Source: thesis metadata and abstract.

name: Slide title

text: Applications run Apollon. Artemis, VS Code, and Athena integrate it - in that order of importance here.
textPreview: Applications run Apollon. Artemis, VS Code, and Athena integrate it - in that order of importance here.

alt: Apollon component model with applications, shared library, and integrations

text: 0:15-0:50. Use the diagram's three columns. Standalone and mobile are Apollon applications. Artemis, VS Code, and Athena are integrations; Artemis is the primary one. The standalone conversion service is intentionally absent because it is an internal server component, not a peer environment. Apollon JSON: presentation-research/diagrams/apollon-products-and-integrations.json.

text: The hard part was reusing improvements without breaking host workflows.
textPreview: The hard part was reusing improvements without breaking host workflows.

text: 0:50-1:30. Tell this as one connected problem. Editing friction affected individual modeling; missing awareness affected teams; Artemis migration turned shared-editor changes into compatibility work. Source: thesis introduction, requirements, and implementation chapters.

alt: Remote selection, drag highlighting, and named cursor

text: 3:15-3:55. Tell the UI evolution in three steps: remote selection and drag highlights, named live cursors, then viewport following. These signals expose intent before the final model update arrives. The same awareness surface is reusable in standalone shared sessions and Artemis team modeling. Source: thesis collaboration implementation.

text: 3:55-4:25. The communication model explains why awareness belongs beside, not inside, the diagram model. Yjs synchronizes model state; awareness messages describe the current session and disappear with it. Apollon JSON: figures/standalone-collaboration-communication.json.

text: The boundary that matters: Artemis workflows reuse one maintained Apollon library.

text: 7:25-8:00. Artemis owns modeling exercises, quiz data, assessment records, permissions, persistence, and team sessions. Apollon provides the diagram editor, model and renderer, and collaboration-awareness UI. The conversion service is not a peer integration and is deliberately absent. Apollon JSON: presentation-research/diagrams/artemis-apollon-integration-focus.json.
